_meta.description: Atlanta GA Metro — Manual URL Curation Results for scrape_registry ingestion
_meta.session_context: Same methodology as Portland OR and Denver/CO Springs files. Covers core Atlanta metro (Fulton, DeKalb, Gwinnett, Cobb, Clayton, Cherokee, Forsyth, Henry, Rockdale, Douglas, Paulding counties). PWSIDs not yet resolved — Claude Code must look them up via SDWIS query before inserting.
target_areas: Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell MSA core (Fulton, DeKalb, Gwinnett, Cobb, Clayton counties)
Outer Atlanta metro (Cherokee, Forsyth, Henry, Rockdale, Douglas, Paulding counties)
ga_context_notes: Georgia water utilities are primarily county-level authorities or county departments — unlike Colorado's special district fragmentation. Each county typically has one dominant water provider covering most of the county, often branded as a 'Water Authority' or county department.

All utilities within the Metropolitan North Georgia Water Planning District (MNGWPD) are required by state law to have a tiered rate structure for residential water to encourage conservation. This covers essentially all of the Atlanta metro.
Georgia utilities are NOT regulated by the Georgia PSC (which covers electric/gas only). Rates are set locally by county commissions or independent authority boards.
Atlanta DWM is unusual: rates have been frozen since 2012 and are funded partly by Municipal Option Sales Tax (MOST). Rate freeze extends through June 2028.
DeKalb County is in an active consent decree with EPA and Georgia EPD for sewer overflows — this is the primary driver of its aggressive 10%/yr rate increases through 2034.
Billing convention: Atlanta DWM bills in CCF (hundred cubic feet). Most other GA utilities bill in 1,000 gallons. Check per-utility — this matters for scraper parsing.

Georgia sewer billing: unlike CO's Winter Quarter Average, Georgia utilities typically calculate sewer charges based on actual monthly water consumption (not a winter average baseline), unless otherwise noted.
Metropolitan North Georgia Water Planning District membership required tiered rates; this is confirmed active across all 12 utilities in this file.
_meta.insert_sql_template: INSERT INTO utility.scrape_registry (pwsid, url, url_source, status, notes) VALUES (:pwsid, :url, 'curated_atlanta', 'pending', 'Atlanta metro manual curation 2026-03-26') ON CONFLICT (pwsid, url) DO NOTHING;
_meta.pwsid_lookup_sql: SELECT s.pwsid, s.pws_name, s.city, s.state_code, s.population_served_count FROM utility.sdwis_systems s WHERE s.state_code = 'GA' AND s.pws_type_code = 'CWS' AND (UPPER(s.pws_name) LIKE :name_pattern OR UPPER(s.city) = :city) ORDER BY s.population_served_count DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 5;
_meta.pwsid_resolution_strategy: Query SDWIS using sdwis_name_hint OR sdwis_city (not AND). GA utilities often appear under formal authority names (e.g., 'HENRY COUNTY WATER AUTHORITY') distinct from colloquial names. Confirm population_served_count is plausible before inserting.
coverage_notes: 3 core Fulton/DeKalb entries (Atlanta DWM, DeKalb Watershed, Fulton County Water)
3 immediate ring entries (Gwinnett DWR, Cobb County Water, Clayton County Water Authority)
2 north metro entries (Cherokee CCWSA, Forsyth County Water & Sewer)
3 outer ring entries (Henry County Water Authority, Rockdale Water Resources, Paulding County Water)
2 supplemental entries (Gwinnett rates PDF direct, Paulding 2025 rate PDF)

utilities.utility_name: City of Atlanta Department of Watershed Management
utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates frozen since 2012 through June 30, 2028. Funded by Municipal Option Sales Tax (MOST), conservation, and fiscal planning — no rate increases needed. Base charge $6.56/mo for water inside and outside city, and for sewer. Tiered structure with different inside-city vs. outside-city volumetric rates. Bills in CCF (hundred cubic feet), NOT 1,000 gallons — unusual for GA. Serves ~1.2 million customers daily. Rate freeze memo effective Jul 1, 2024 through Jun 30, 2028.
utilities.pwsid_note: Atlanta DWM serves both inside-city and outside-city customers. Outside-city rates are higher per CCF. Also operates wholesale agreements with surrounding counties. Serves portions of Fulton and DeKalb counties.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 10% annual rate increases for 10 years approved March 11, 2025. Schedule: first 10% increase effective July 1, 2025 (per AJC, first increase hit August 2025 after delay); then 10% each Jan 1 from 2026 through 2034. Customers will see ~20% total increase by Jan 1, 2026. Avg monthly bill currently ~$70/mo → ~$180/mo by 2034. Capital plan: $4.3B over 10 years. Driven by EPA/Georgia EPD consent decree for sewer overflows (950+ water main breaks in 2024; 40% of system will be 70+ years old by 2030). Scott Candler Water Treatment Plant is DeKalb's only water treatment facility — single-source vulnerability. Affordability program launched Aug 2025 capping bills for low-income residents.
utilities.pwsid_note: DeKalb is under active EPA/EPD consent decree. Single water source (Chattahoochee River via Scott Candler WTP). Typical residential usage 4,000 gal/mo. Sewer billed on actual monthly water consumption.

utilities.utility_name: Gwinnett County Department of Water Resources
utilities.current_rate_summary: 4.5% increase effective Jan 1, 2025 per 10-year rate resolution (2021–2031). Rate structure: increases in odd years only. 3-tier residential structure: Tier 1 (0–8,000 gal) base rate; Tier 2 (8,001–12,000 gal) 1.5× tier 1; Tier 3 (above 12,000 gal) 2.0× tier 1. Summer surcharge (Jun–Oct) on usage above 125% of prior Jan–Mar avg. Avg household at 4,500 gal: ~$80.48/mo (water+sewer). Bills in 1,000 gallons. Monthly billing. Source: Lake Lanier (Buford Dam/Chattahoochee). Serves ~180,000 wastewater customers, 3,000+ miles of pipeline.
utilities.rate_increase_schedule: 4.5% in odd years through 2031 per 2021 resolution
utilities.pwsid_note: Largest water utility in Georgia by population served outside Atlanta DWM. Source: Lake Lanier. Rate PDF direct link confirmed for 2025 schedule (11 pages). Customer care page links to current rates table.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2.5% water / 3.5% wastewater rate increase effective April 1, 2025 (approved Dec 10, 2024). Annual 3.5% increases in water and wastewater for 2026–2029 (effective Jan 1 each year). Avg residential customer uses 5,000 gal/mo, pays $67.30/mo total (water+sewer+stormwater). +$1.90/mo from 2025 increase. Lowest rates in metro Atlanta. Up to 10% of Water System revenue may transfer to County General Fund (FY25: 5% transferred). Cities inside Cobb (Acworth, Austell, Kennesaw, Powder Springs, Marietta, Smyrna) pay slightly lower rates as no stormwater services. Monthly billing. Rate PDF effective April 1, 2025 confirmed.
utilities.pwsid_note: Cobb County buys wholesale from Cobb-Marietta Water Authority then retails. Lowest rates in metro Atlanta per county comparison data. Monthly billing since confirmed on rate page.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates effective January 1, 2025 confirmed on page (explicit statement on rates-fees-billing page). Monthly billing. Does not receive any tax dollars — funded entirely by water, sewer, and stormwater revenues. Base rates cover fixed costs; tiered volume charges. 8% rate increase effective January 1, 2026 (announced on iminvested page — rates remain lower than most in the region even with increase). Stormwater fee included in bill.
utilities.pwsid_note: Independent water authority — not a county department. No tax revenue. Serves Clayton County (including portions of Fayette and Henry counties). Commercial rates on separate page at ccwa.us.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 5% increase Jan 1, 2025 (part of 2023–2025 series approved Jan 2023). Bimonthly service charge + monthly volume charge. 3-tiered residential conservation rate structure; baseline set by winter average (Nov 1–Mar 31) consumption. Summer (Apr 1–Oct 31) inclining block structure. Base charge avg $10.71/mo on 5,000 gal meter in 2025 (up from $10.20). 24% of sewer customers do not receive Fulton County water (on septic or other supply). North Fulton: 55.6% of sewer customers. New 5% increases approved Oct 1, 2025 for Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 1, 2027. Note: distinct from City of Atlanta DWM — Fulton County serves unincorporated north Fulton and south Fulton areas.
utilities.pwsid_note: Separate from City of Atlanta DWM (id=1). Serves unincorporated Fulton County (North Fulton: Alpharetta, Roswell, Milton areas; South Fulton: Fairburn, Union City, Palmetto areas). Customer service: 404-612-6830.

utilities.utility_name: Cherokee County Water and Sewerage Authority
utilities.current_rate_summary: Rate increase effective September 1, 2025 — 3rd rate increase in last 10 years (prior: Jan 2019, Apr 2023). Remainder (above threshold gallons) at $8.45/1,000 gal water; $8.00/1,000 gal sewer. 3/4" meter residential rate chart for 0–40,000 gallons in 2025 PDF. Street light charge increased from $3.50 to $4.00/mo (first in 10+ years). Monthly billing. Public not-for-profit governmental entity. Not part of Cherokee County government and receives no tax revenue. Source: Etowah River and Hollis Q. Lathem Reservoir (Yellow Creek). 84,000+ meters, 225,000+ persons. Also supplies wholesale water to Bartow County, Pickens County, Dawson County, City of Ball Ground, City of Jasper, City of Waleska, City of Canton.
utilities.pwsid_note: Also check: 'CHEROKEE COUNTY WATER AND SEWERAGE AUTHORITY' in SDWIS. Large system — serves more than 225,000. Also operates a Salacoa Area Water System (separate small system in NE Cherokee County, may have its own PWSID).

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rate schedule PDF confirmed (effective Jan 1, 2025). Hydrant Meter Policy ratified Aug 21, 2025. Rate resolution document available. 2026 rate schedule PDF also confirmed. County department of Forsyth County government. Fastest-growing county in Georgia and one of fastest in US; significant infrastructure investment pressures. AutoPay discount: one month penalty waived upon enrollment.
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: City of Cumming also has its own water utility (Cumming Utilities) that is separate from Forsyth County Water & Sewer. SDWIS may list both — verify carefully. Forsyth County Water serves unincorporated county areas; City of Cumming utility serves inside Cumming city limits.

utilities.current_rate_summary: City of Cumming has its own water utility serving inside city limits (distinct from Forsyth County Water & Sewer). Rate comparison study on cummingutilities.com site provides regional benchmark data but city page itself was not directly searched for 2025 rates. Flag for secondary verification.
utilities.pwsid_note: Distinct small city system within Forsyth County. Rates typically lower inside city limits than Forsyth County outside. Confirmed PWSID lookup will likely separate these from Forsyth County Water.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Tiered rate structure (required by MNGWPD). Monthly base charge (by meter size) + tiered volumetric charges. Rates uniform inside Henry County; outside county customers pay 1.25× multiplier. Rate PDF linked from rates page. Founded 1961. Monthly billing. Note: domain is hcwa.com (Henry County Water Authority, McDonough GA) — distinct from hcwd2.com (Henry County Water District #2, different entity) and myhcwa.com (Heard County Water Authority, different county entirely).
utilities.pwsid_note: Confirm PWSID is for Henry County Water Authority at hcwa.com, not Henry County Water District 2 (hcwd2.com) — these are distinct systems within Henry County.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rockdale Water Resources is a department of Rockdale County Government. Rate increase effective January 1, 2026 (notice published Dec 2025). 2025 rates were the prior level — exact 2025 schedule needs direct fetch of fees page. Monthly billing. The fees page at rockdalewaterresources.com/fees/ covers connection fees and service charges; full volumetric rate table may be at separate URL. Flag for secondary verification of 2025 rate amounts.
utilities.pwsid_note: County department, not independent authority. SDWIS may list as 'ROCKDALE COUNTY' or 'ROCKDALE WATER RESOURCES'. Smaller county (~95,000 pop). Also try 'ROCKDALE COUNTY WATER AND SEWERAGE AUTHORITY' from historical references.

utilities.utility_name: Douglasville-Douglas County Water and Sewer Authority
utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates page confirmed active. Sewer billed at 80% of water consumption (WSA acknowledges not all water returns to sewer). All Douglas County properties — including those on wells — charged a stormwater fee ($4.00/ERU). Inside city of Douglasville: stormwater + garbage pickup billed as single charge (WSA acts as billing agent for city sanitation). Senior/disabled discount: reduced water base charge $2.50 + sewer base charge $2.50. Monthly billing. Credit card fee: 2.95% (effective Jun 20, 2025). Dog River is the primary water source (reservoir capacity ~2 billion gallons). Established 1985/1986. ~42,000 customers, ~100,000 residents. Multiple EPA/state awards for treatment excellence.
utilities.pwsid_note: Independent authority serving Douglas County and city of Douglasville. Also try 'DOUGLAS COUNTY WATER SEWER AUTHORITY' in SDWIS.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rate schedule PDF confirmed at paulding.gov. Monthly billing. Leak insurance included with all residential accounts. City of Dallas (inside Paulding) transferred its water/sewer billing to Paulding County Water System effective August 22, 2025. Previously, City of Dallas had its own sewer rates (billed by Paulding on Dallas's behalf for some areas). Now consolidated under Paulding. Office: 3844 Atlanta Hwy, Hiram, GA 30141. 24-hr emergency: 770-445-2761.
utilities.pwsid_note: County department. Note: City of Dallas transferred its water billing to Paulding County Water Aug 22, 2025 — SDWIS may still list City of Dallas as a separate PWS. Verify which PWSID covers the Dallas service area post-transfer.

utilities.utility_name: Gwinnett County DWR — 2025 Rate Schedule PDF (secondary URL)
utilities.current_rate_summary: Direct link to 11-page 2025 Rates and Fee Schedule PDF. Contains full residential, commercial, multi-family, irrigation and builder rate tables plus system development charges. Scraper should target this PDF for structured rate data extraction in addition to the main customer care rates page (id=3).
utilities.pwsid_note: Secondary URL for id=3 — same PWSID, do not insert twice. Deduplicate before insert. Insert with notes appending '[pdf]'.

not_found_or_excluded.note: Wholesale-only authority supplying Cobb County Water System and City of Marietta. No direct retail customers; no PWSID for residential billing purposes. Do not attempt to insert — rate data belongs to retail entities (Cobb County Water System, City of Marietta).

City of Marietta has its own water utility serving inside Marietta city limits (within Cobb County). Separate from Cobb County Water System. Rate page likely at mariettaga.gov. Not searched in this session — add to future curation pass.
Roswell is in north Fulton County but has its own municipal water system (not Fulton County Water). Rate page likely at roswellgov.com. Not searched this session.
Sandy Springs is a large incorporated city in north Fulton County. May receive water from Fulton County Water (id=6) or have its own system. Service provider relationship needs verification.
Alpharetta has its own water system in north Fulton County. Rate page at alpharetta.ga.us. Not searched this session.

not_found_or_excluded.utility_name: Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority
not_found_or_excluded.note: Serves Newton County east of Rockdale. Adjacent to metro but generally outside MSA core. Not searched this session.
Receives some wholesale water from CCWSA (Cherokee). Own retail rates. Outside MSA core. Not searched this session.

not_found_or_excluded.utility_name: Henry County Water and Sewer Authority (hcwsa.com)
not_found_or_excluded.note: Appears to be a different/alternate entity from Henry County Water Authority (hcwa.com). SDWIS lookup will clarify whether these are one entity or two. Domain hcwsa.com appeared in search results with 1.5× outside-county multiplier vs hcwa.com's 1.25× — verify carefully.

georgia_structural_notes: Unlike Colorado's fragmented special district structure, Georgia metros typically have one dominant county-level water authority or department per county. The SDWIS PWSID lookup is generally cleaner — one county → one primary PWSID for the main system.

Metropolitan North Georgia Water Planning District (MNGWPD): All utilities in the 15-county MNGWPD service area are required to have tiered residential water rates. This includes all utilities in this file. Tier multipliers may vary (Gwinnett uses 1.5×/2.0×; others vary), but tiered structure is universal.

DeKalb County consent decree: DeKalb is under active EPA/Georgia EPD consent decree for sewer system overflows. The 10%/yr rate increases for 10 years ($4.3B total CIP) are legally driven. This is the most aggressive rate trajectory in metro Atlanta by far.

Atlanta DWM rate freeze: Rates frozen since 2012 through June 2028 — the longest freeze in any major metro utility examined in this project. MOST (Municipal Option Sales Tax) provides significant non-rate revenue. Scraper results showing no rate change are correct and expected.

Georgia sewer calculation: Most GA utilities bill sewer based on actual monthly water consumption (not Colorado's Winter Quarter Average). Atlanta DWM and a few others may use different methods — check each utility's method.

Billing units: Atlanta DWM bills in CCF (hundred cubic feet). All others in this file bill in gallons (per 1,000 gallons). This is critical for rate scraper parsing — ATL DWM rate data will be in $/CCF not $/1,000 gal.

Forsyth County vs. City of Cumming: Two separate water systems within Forsyth County. Forsyth County Water & Sewer (unincorporated) and City of Cumming Utilities (inside city limits) coexist. Both have PWSIDs. SDWIS will distinguish them by city name.

City of Dallas / Paulding County transition: City of Dallas transferred its water/sewer billing to Paulding County Water System effective Aug 22, 2025. SDWIS may still list a City of Dallas PWSID — check if it was deactivated post-transfer.

Authority vs. county department distinction: Clayton, Cherokee, Rockdale (historically as W&S Authority), Henry, and Douglas are organized as independent authorities. Fulton, DeKalb, Cobb, Gwinnett, Forsyth, and Paulding are county departments. This distinction affects which SDWIS name pattern to search.
action_checklist: 1. Run SDWIS query for all GA CWS PWSIDs with population > 1,000 in Fulton, DeKalb, Gwinnett, Cobb, Clayton, Cherokee, Forsyth, Henry, Rockdale, Douglas, Paulding counties.
2. For id=14 (Gwinnett PDF secondary URL): confirm same PWSID as id=3, insert with notes '[pdf]'. Do not create a separate PWSID entry.
3. For id=9 (City of Cumming): SDWIS lookup using city='CUMMING' and name_hint='CITY OF CUMMING'. Confirm separate PWSID from Forsyth County Water (id=8).
4. For id=11 (Rockdale): Fetch rockdalewaterresources.com/fees/ to confirm 2025 volumetric rate structure. Insert as 'url_unconfirmed' until direct fetch succeeds.
5. For DeKalb (id=2): confirm rates page URL actually shows current 2025 rate table (the page may link to a PDF). If PDF, capture PDF URL as secondary URL.
6. For Paulding (id=13): verify whether SDWIS shows a separate City of Dallas PWSID post-Aug 2025 billing transfer. May need to deactivate or merge entry.

7. NOT_FOUND entries to research in a follow-up pass: City of Marietta, City of Roswell, City of Sandy Springs, City of Alpharetta — all are significant municipalities within the metro that have or receive municipal water service.

9. Check for Henry County: confirm hcwa.com vs. hcwsa.com entity relationship. SDWIS lookup may return both 'HENRY COUNTY WATER AUTHORITY' and 'HENRY COUNTY WATER AND SEWER AUTHORITY' — verify they are separate PWSIDs before inserting both.
10. Gwinnett 2025 rate PDF (id=14 / secondary to id=3): scraper should be configured to attempt PDF text extraction for structured rate tables, not just HTML parsing.
